81	# `_spbase` is a subclass of `SparseABC`.
82	# This allows other submodules to check for instances of sparse subclasses
83	# via `scipy._lib._sparse.issparse`, without introducing
84	# an import dependency on `scipy.sparse`.
85	class _spbase(SparseABC):
86	    """ This class provides a base class for all sparse array and matrix classes.
87	    It cannot be instantiated.  Most of the work is provided by subclasses.
88	    """
89	
90	    __array_priority__ = 10.1
91	    _format = 'und'  # undefined
92	    _allow_nd: tuple[int, ...] = (2,)
93	
94	    @property
95	    def ndim(self) -> int:
96	        return len(self._shape)
97	
98	    @property
99	    def _shape_as_2d(self):
100	        s = self._shape
101	        return (1, s[-1]) if len(s) == 1 else s
102	
103	    @property
104	    def _bsr_container(self):
105	        from ._bsr import bsr_array
106	        return bsr_array
107	
108	    @property
109	    def _coo_container(self):
110	        from ._coo import coo_array
111	        return coo_array
112	
113	    @property
114	    def _csc_container(self):
115	        from ._csc import csc_array
116	        return csc_array
117	
118	    @property
119	    def _csr_container(self):
120	        from ._csr import csr_array
121	        return csr_array
122	
123	    @property
124	    def _dia_container(self):
125	        from ._dia import dia_array
126	        return dia_array
127	
128	    @property
129	    def _dok_container(self):
130	        from ._dok import dok_array
131	        return dok_array
132	
133	    @property
134	    def _lil_container(self):
135	        from ._lil import lil_array
136	        return lil_array
137	
138	    def __init__(self, arg1, *, maxprint=None):
139	        self._shape = None
140	        if self.__class__.__name__ == '_spbase':
141	            raise ValueError("This class is not intended"
142	                             " to be instantiated directly.")
143	        if isinstance(self, sparray) and np.isscalar(arg1):
144	            raise ValueError(
145	                "scipy sparse array classes do not support instantiation from a scalar"
146	            )
147	        self.maxprint = MAXPRINT if maxprint is None else maxprint
148	
149	    @property
150	    def shape(self):
151	        return self._shape
152	
153	    def reshape(self, *shape, order="C", copy=False):
154	        """reshape(self, shape, order='C', copy=False)
155	
156	        Gives a new shape to a sparse array/matrix without changing its data.
157	
158	        Parameters
159	        ----------
160	        *shape : int
161	            Integers specifying the new shape.
162	            The new shape should be compatible with the original shape.
163	        order : {'C', 'F'}, optional
164	            Read the elements using this index order. 'C' means to read and
165	            write the elements using C-like index order; e.g., read entire first
166	            row, then second row, etc. 'F' means to read and write the elements
167	            using Fortran-like index order; e.g., read entire first column, then
168	            second column, etc.
169	        copy : bool, optional
170	            Indicates whether or not attributes of self should be copied
171	            whenever possible. The degree to which attributes are copied varies
172	            depending on the type of sparse array being used.
173	
174	        Returns
175	        -------
176	        reshaped : sparse array/matrix
177	            A sparse array/matrix with the given `shape`, not necessarily of the same
178	            format as the current object.
179	
180	        See Also
181	        --------
182	        numpy.reshape : NumPy's implementation of 'reshape' for ndarrays
183	        """
184	        # If the shape already matches, don't bother doing an actual reshape
185	        # Otherwise, the default is to convert to COO and use its reshape
186	        # Don't restrict ndim on this first call. That happens in constructor
187	        shape = check_shape(shape, self.shape, allow_nd=range(1, 65))
188	        if shape == self.shape:
189	            if copy:
190	                return self.copy()
191	            else:
192	                return self
193	
194	        return self.tocoo(copy=copy).reshape(shape, order=order, copy=False)
195	
196	    def resize(self, *shape):
197	        """Resize the array/matrix in-place to dimensions given by ``shape``.
198	
199	        Any elements that lie within the new shape will remain at the same
200	        indices, while non-zero elements lying outside the new shape are
201	        removed.
202	
203	        Parameters
204	        ----------
205	        *shape : int
206	            number of rows and columns in the new array/matrix
207	
208	        Notes
209	        -----
210	        The semantics are not identical to `numpy.ndarray.resize` or
211	        `numpy.resize`. Here, the same data will be maintained at each index
212	        before and after reshape, if that index is within the new bounds. In
213	        numpy, resizing maintains contiguity of the array, moving elements
214	        around in the logical array but not within a flattened representation.
215	
216	        We give no guarantees about whether the underlying data attributes
217	        (arrays, etc.) will be modified in place or replaced with new objects.
218	        """
219	        # As an inplace operation, this requires implementation in each format.
220	        raise NotImplementedError(
221	            f'{type(self).__name__}.resize is not implemented')
222	
223	    def astype(self, dtype, casting='unsafe', copy=True):
224	        """Cast the array/matrix elements to a specified type.
225	
226	        Parameters
227	        ----------
228	        dtype : str or numpy dtype
229	            Typecode or data-type to which to cast the data.
230	        casting : {'no', 'equiv', 'safe', 'same_kind', 'unsafe'}, optional
231	            Controls what kind of data casting may occur.
232	            Defaults to 'unsafe' for backwards compatibility.
233	            'no' means the data types should not be cast at all.
234	            'equiv' means only byte-order changes are allowed.
235	            'safe' means only casts which can preserve values are allowed.
236	            'same_kind' means only safe casts or casts within a kind,
237	            like float64 to float32, are allowed.
238	            'unsafe' means any data conversions may be done.
239	        copy : bool, optional
240	            If `copy` is `False`, the result might share some memory with this
241	            array/matrix. If `copy` is `True`, it is guaranteed that the result and
242	            this array/matrix do not share any memory.
243	
244	        Returns
245	        -------
246	        array/matrix
247	            This array/matrix with data type `dtype`.
248	        """
249	
250	        dtype = getdtype(dtype)
251	        if self.dtype != dtype:
252	            return self.tocsr().astype(
253	                dtype, casting=casting, copy=copy).asformat(self.format)
254	        elif copy:
255	            return self.copy()
256	        else:
257	            return self
258	
259	    @classmethod
260	    def _ascontainer(cls, X, **kwargs):
261	        if issubclass(cls, sparray):
262	            return np.asarray(X, **kwargs)
263	        else:
264	            return asmatrix(X, **kwargs)
265	
266	    @classmethod
267	    def _container(cls, X, **kwargs):
268	        if issubclass(cls, sparray):
269	            return np.array(X, **kwargs)
270	        else:
271	            return matrix(X, **kwargs)
272	
273	    def _asfptype(self):
274	        """Upcast array to a floating point format (if necessary)"""
275	
276	        fp_types = ['f', 'd', 'F', 'D']
277	
278	        if self.dtype.char in fp_types:
279	            return self
280	        else:
281	            for fp_type in fp_types:
282	                if self.dtype <= np.dtype(fp_type):
283	                    return self.astype(fp_type, copy=False)
284	
285	            raise TypeError(
286	                f'cannot upcast [{self.dtype.name}] to a floating point format'
287	            )
288	
289	    def __iter__(self):
290	        for r in range(self.shape[0]):
291	            yield self[r]
292	
293	    def _getmaxprint(self):
294	        """Maximum number of elements to display when printed."""
295	        return self.maxprint
296	
297	    def count_nonzero(self, axis=None):
298	        """Number of non-zero entries.
299	
300	        This is equivalent to::
301	
302	            np.count_nonzero(a.toarray(), axis=axis)
303	
304	        Unlike the nnz property, which return the number of stored
305	        entries (the length of the data attribute), this method counts the
306	        actual number of non-zero entries in data.
307	
308	        Duplicate entries are summed before counting.
309	
310	        Parameters
311	        ----------
312	        axis : {-2, -1, 0, 1, None} optional
313	            Count nonzeros for the whole array, or along a specified axis.
314	
315	            .. versionadded:: 1.15.0
316	
317	        Returns
318	        -------
319	        numpy array
320	            A reduced array (no axis `axis`) holding the number of nonzero values
321	            for each of the indices of the nonaxis dimensions.
322	
323	        Notes
324	        -----
325	        If you want to count nonzero and explicit zero stored values (e.g. nnz)
326	        along an axis, two fast idioms are provided by `numpy` functions for the
327	        common CSR, CSC, COO formats.
328	
329	        For the major axis in CSR (rows) and CSC (cols) use `np.diff`:
330	
331	            >>> import numpy as np
332	            >>> import scipy as sp
333	            >>> A = sp.sparse.csr_array([[4, 5, 0], [7, 0, 0]])
334	            >>> major_axis_stored_values = np.diff(A.indptr)  # -> np.array([2, 1])
335	
336	        For the minor axis in CSR (cols) and CSC (rows) use `numpy.bincount` with
337	        minlength ``A.shape[1]`` for CSR and ``A.shape[0]`` for CSC:
338	
339	            >>> csr_minor_stored_values = np.bincount(A.indices, minlength=A.shape[1])
340	
341	        For COO, use the minor axis approach for either `axis`:
342	
343	            >>> A = A.tocoo()
344	            >>> coo_axis0_stored_values = np.bincount(A.coords[0], minlength=A.shape[1])
345	            >>> coo_axis1_stored_values = np.bincount(A.coords[1], minlength=A.shape[0])
346	
347	        Examples
348	        --------
349	
350	            >>> A = sp.sparse.csr_array([[4, 5, 0], [7, 0, 0]])
351	            >>> A.count_nonzero(axis=0)
352	            array([2, 1, 0])
353	        """
354	        clsname = self.__class__.__name__
355	        raise NotImplementedError(f"count_nonzero not implemented for {clsname}.")
356	
357	    def _getnnz(self, axis=None):
358	        """Number of stored values, including explicit zeros.
359	
360	        Parameters
361	        ----------
362	        axis : {-2, -1, 0, 1, None} optional
363	            Report stored values for the whole array, or along a specified axis.
364	
365	        See Also
366	        --------
367	        count_nonzero : Number of non-zero entries
368	        """
369	        clsname = self.__class__.__name__
370	        raise NotImplementedError(f"getnnz not implemented for {clsname}.")
371	
372	    @property
373	    def nnz(self) -> int:
374	        """Number of stored values, including explicit zeros.
375	
376	        See Also
377	        --------
378	        count_nonzero : Number of non-zero entries
379	        """
380	        return self._getnnz()
381	
382	    @property
383	    def size(self) -> int:
384	        """Number of stored values.
385	
386	        See Also
387	        --------
388	        count_nonzero : Number of non-zero values.
389	        """
390	        return self._getnnz()
391	
392	    @property
393	    def format(self) -> str:
394	        """Format string for matrix."""
395	        return self._format
396	
397	    @property
398	    def T(self):
399	        """Transpose."""
400	        return self.transpose()
401	    
402	    @property
403	    def mT(self):
404	        """Matrix transpose.
405	        
406	        See Also
407	        --------
408	        scipy.sparse.matrix_transpose : equivalent function
409	        """
410	        if (n := self.ndim) < 2:
411	            raise ValueError(f"Array must be at least 2-dimensional, but it is {n}-D")
412	        assert n == 2
413	        return self.transpose()
414	
415	    @property
416	    def real(self):
417	        return self._real()
418	
419	    @property
420	    def imag(self):
421	        return self._imag()
422	
423	    def __repr__(self):
424	        _, format_name = _formats[self.format]
425	        sparse_cls = 'array' if isinstance(self, sparray) else 'matrix'
426	        return (
427	            f"<{format_name} sparse {sparse_cls} of dtype '{self.dtype}'\n"
428	            f"\twith {self.nnz} stored elements and shape {self.shape}>"
429	        )
430	
431	    def __str__(self):
432	        maxprint = self._getmaxprint()
433	
434	        A = self.tocoo()
435	
436	        # helper function, outputs "(i,j)  v"
437	        def tostr(coords, data):
438	            pairs = zip(zip(*(c.tolist() for c in coords)), data)
439	            return '\n'.join(f'  {idx}\t{val}' for idx, val in pairs)
440	
441	        out = repr(self)
442	        if self.nnz == 0:
443	            return out
444	
445	        out += '\n  Coords\tValues\n'
446	        if self.nnz > maxprint:
447	            half = maxprint // 2
448	            out += tostr(tuple(c[:half] for c in A.coords), A.data[:half])
449	            out += "\n  :\t:\n"
450	            half = maxprint - half
451	            out += tostr(tuple(c[-half:] for c in A.coords), A.data[-half:])
452	        else:
453	            out += tostr(A.coords, A.data)
454	
455	        return out
456	
457	    def __bool__(self):  # Simple -- other ideas?
458	        if self.shape == (1, 1):
459	            return self.nnz != 0
460	        else:
461	            raise ValueError("The truth value of an array with more than one "
462	                             "element is ambiguous. Use a.any() or a.all().")
463	
464	    # What should len(sparse) return? For consistency with dense matrices,
465	    # perhaps it should be the number of rows?  But for some uses the number of
466	    # non-zeros is more important.  For now, raise an exception!
467	    def __len__(self):
468	        raise TypeError("sparse array length is ambiguous; use getnnz()"
469	                        " or shape[0]")
470	
471	    def asformat(self, format, copy=False):
472	        """Return this array/matrix in the passed format.
473	
474	        Parameters
475	        ----------
476	        format : {str, None}
477	            The desired sparse format ("csr", "csc", "lil", "dok", "array", ...)
478	            or None for no conversion.
479	        copy : bool, optional
480	            If True, the result is guaranteed to not share data with self.
481	
482	        Returns
483	        -------
484	        A : array/matrix
485	            This array/matrix in the passed format.
486	        """
487	        if format is None or format == self.format:
488	            if copy:
489	                return self.copy()
490	            else:
491	                return self
492	        else:
493	            try:
494	                convert_method = getattr(self, 'to' + format)
495	            except AttributeError as e:
496	                raise ValueError(f'Format {format} is unknown.') from e
497	
498	            # Forward the copy kwarg, if it's accepted.
499	            try:
500	                return convert_method(copy=copy)
501	            except TypeError:
502	                return convert_method()
503	
504	    ###################################################################
505	    #  NOTE: All arithmetic operations use csr_matrix by default.
506	    # Therefore a new sparse array format just needs to define a
507	    # .tocsr() method to provide arithmetic support. Any of these
508	    # methods can be overridden for efficiency.
509	    ####################################################################
510	
511	    def multiply(self, other):
512	        """Element-wise multiplication by another array/matrix.
513	
514	        Parameters
515	        ----------
516	        other : sparse array or array_like
517	            Array to multiply by.
518	
519	        Returns
520	        -------
521	        sparse array/matrix
522	            Result of element-wise multiplication.
523	        """
524	        if isscalarlike(other):
525	            return self._mul_scalar(other)
526	
527	        if self.ndim < 3:
528	            try:
529	                return self._multiply_2d_with_broadcasting(other)
530	            except AttributeError:
531	                return self.tocsr()._multiply_2d_with_broadcasting(other)
532	
533	        if not (issparse(other) or isdense(other)):
534	            # If it's a list or whatever, treat it like an array
535	            other_a = np.asanyarray(other)
536	            if other_a.ndim == 0 and other_a.dtype == np.object_:
537	                # numpy creates a 0d object array if all else fails.
538	                # Not interpretable as an array; return NotImplemented so
539	                # other's __rmul__ can kick in if that's implemented.
540	                return NotImplemented
541	            # Allow custom sparse class indicated by attr sparse gh-6520
542	            try:
543	                other.shape
544	            except AttributeError:
545	                other = other_a
546	
547	        if self.shape != other.shape:
548	            raise ValueError("inconsistent shapes: >2D multiply() does not yet "
549	                             "support broadcasting")
550	
551	        # self is >2D so must be COO
552	        if isdense(other):
553	            data = np.multiply(self.data, other[self.coords])
554	            result = self.copy()
555	            result.data = data.view(np.ndarray).ravel()
556	            return result
557	
558	        elif issparse(other):
559	            csr_self = self.reshape(1, -1).tocsr()
560	            csr_other = other.reshape(1, -1).tocsr()
561	            return csr_self._binopt(csr_other, '_elmul_').reshape(self.shape)
562	
563	        else:
564	            # Not scalar, dense or sparse. Return NotImplemented so that
565	            # other's __rmul__ can kick in if that's implemented.
566	            return NotImplemented
567	
568	    def _maximum_minimum(self, other, np_op):
569	        if not (issparse(other) or isdense(other) or isscalarlike(other)):
570	            # If it's a list or whatever, treat it like an array
571	            other_a = np.asanyarray(other)
572	            if other_a.ndim == 0 and other_a.dtype == np.object_:
573	                # numpy creates a 0d object array if all else fails.
574	                # We don't know how to handle it either.
575	                raise NotImplementedError('maximum or minimum with an unrecognized '
576	                                          'array type is not supported')
577	            # Allow custom sparse class indicated by attr sparse gh-6520
578	            try:
579	                other.shape
580	            except AttributeError:
581	                other = other_a
582	
583	        if isscalarlike(other):
584	            if np_op(0, other):
585	                pos_neg = 'positive' if np_op == np.maximum else 'negative'
586	                warn(f"Taking {np_op.__name__} with a {pos_neg} number results in a"
587	                     " dense matrix.", SparseEfficiencyWarning, stacklevel=3)
588	                return self.__class__(np_op(self.toarray(), other))
589	            else:
590	                if self.ndim < 3:
591	                    cs_self = self if self.format in ('csr', 'csc') else self.tocsr()
592	                    return cs_self._scalar_binopt(other, np_op)
593	                csr_self = self.reshape(1, -1).tocsr()
594	                result = csr_self._scalar_binopt(other, np_op)
595	                return result.tocoo().reshape(self.shape)
596	        elif isdense(other):
597	            return np_op(self.todense(), other)
598	        elif issparse(other):
599	            if self.shape != other.shape:
600	                raise ValueError(f"inconsistent shapes {self.shape=} {other.shape=}")
601	            if self.ndim < 3:  # shape is same so other.ndim < 3
602	                cs_self = self if self.format in ('csr', 'csc') else self.tocsr()
603	                return cs_self._binopt(other, f'_{np_op.__name__}_')
604	            csr_self = self.reshape(1, -1).tocsr()
605	            csr_other = other.reshape(1, -1).tocsr()
606	            result = csr_self._binopt(csr_other, f'_{np_op.__name__}_')
607	            return result.tocoo().reshape(self.shape)
608	        else:
609	            raise ValueError("Operands not compatible.")

611	    def maximum(self, other):
612	        """Element-wise maximum between this and another array/matrix.
613	
614	        Parameters
615	        ----------
616	        other : array_like or sparse array
617	            Array to compare against.
618	
619	        Returns
620	        -------
621	        sparse array/matrix or ndarray
622	            Result of element-wise minimum. The type depends on `other`:
623	
624	            - If `other` is a scalar, the result is a sparse array/matrix.
625	            - If `other` is an ndarray, the result is an ndarray.
626	        """
627	        return self._maximum_minimum(other, np.maximum)

629	    def minimum(self, other):
630	        """Element-wise minimum between this and another array/matrix.
631	
632	        Parameters
633	        ----------
634	        other : array_like or sparse array
635	            Array to compare against.
636	
637	        Returns
638	        -------
639	        sparse array/matrix or ndarray
640	            Result of element-wise minimum. The type depends on `other`:
641	
642	            - If `other` is a scalar, the result is a sparse array/matrix.
643	            - If `other` is an ndarray, the result is an ndarray.
644	        """
645	        return self._maximum_minimum(other, np.minimum)

647	    def dot(self, other):
648	        """Ordinary dot product.
649	
650	        Parameters
651	        ----------
652	        other : array_like or sparse array
653	            Array to take dot product with.
654	
655	        Returns
656	        -------
657	        sparse array/matrix or ndarray
658	            Result of dot product. The type depends on `other`:
659	
660	            - If `other` is a scalar, the result is a sparse array/matrix.
661	            - If `other` is an ndarray, the result is an ndarray.
662	
663	        Examples
664	        --------
665	        >>> import numpy as np
666	        >>> from scipy.sparse import csr_array
667	        >>> A = csr_array([[1, 2, 0], [0, 0, 3], [4, 0, 5]])
668	        >>> v = np.array([1, 0, -1])
669	        >>> A.dot(v)
670	        array([ 1, -3, -1], dtype=int64)
671	
672	        """
673	        if np.isscalar(other):
674	            return self * other
675	        else:
676	            return self @ other

678	    def power(self, n, dtype=None):
679	        """Element-wise power.
680	
681	        Parameters
682	        ----------
683	        n : scalar
684	            `n` is a non-zero scalar (nonzero avoids dense ones creation)
685	            If zero power is desired, special case it to use `numpy.ones`
686	        dtype : dtype, optional
687	            If `dtype` is not specified, the current dtype will be preserved.
688	
689	        Returns
690	        -------
691	        csr array/matrix
692	            Result of raising all elements to the power `n`.
693	        """
694	        return self.tocsr().power(n, dtype=dtype)

696	    def _broadcast_to(self, shape, /, copy=False):
697	        if self.shape == shape:
698	            return self.copy() if copy else self
699	        else:
700	            return self.tocsr()._broadcast_to(shape, copy)

702	    def _comparison(self, other, op):
703	        # We convert to CSR format and use methods _binopt or _scalar_binopt
704	        # If ndim>2 we reshape to 2D, compare and then reshape back to nD
705	        if not (issparse(other) or isdense(other) or isscalarlike(other)):
706	            if is_pydata_spmatrix(other):
707	                # cannot compare with pydata other, but it might compare with us.
708	                return NotImplemented
709	            # If it's a list or whatever, treat it like an array
710	            other_a = np.asanyarray(other)
711	            if other_a.ndim == 0 and other_a.dtype == np.object_:
712	                # numpy creates a 0d object array if all else fails.
713	                # Not interpretable as an array; return NotImplemented so
714	                # other's dunder methods can kick in if implemented.
715	                return NotImplemented
716	            # Allow custom sparse class indicated by attr sparse gh-6520
717	            try:
718	                other.shape
719	            except AttributeError:
720	                other = other_a
721	
722	        if isscalarlike(other):
723	            if not op(0, other):
724	                if np.isnan(other):  # op is not `ne`, so results are all False.
725	                    return self.__class__(self.shape, dtype=np.bool_)
726	                if self.ndim < 3:
727	                    cs_self = self if self.format in ('csc', 'csr') else self.tocsr()
728	                    return cs_self._scalar_binopt(other, op)
729	                csr_self = self.reshape(1, -1).tocsr()
730	                result = csr_self._scalar_binopt(other, op)
731	                return result.tocoo().reshape(self.shape)
732	            else:
733	                warn(f"Comparing a sparse matrix with {other} using {op_sym[op]} "
734	                     f"is inefficient. Try using {op_sym[op_neg[op]]} instead.",
735	                     SparseEfficiencyWarning, stacklevel=3)
736	                if np.isnan(other):
737	                    # op is `ne` cuz op(0, other) and isnan(other). Return all True.
738	                    return self.__class__(np.ones(self.shape, dtype=np.bool_))
739	
740	                # op is eq, le, or ge. Use negated op and then negate.
741	                if self.ndim < 3:
742	                    cs_self = self if self.format in ('csc', 'csr') else self.tocsr()
743	                    inv = cs_self._scalar_binopt(other, op_neg[op])
744	                    all_true = cs_self.__class__(np.ones(cs_self.shape, dtype=np.bool_))
745	                    return all_true - inv
746	
747	                csr_self = self.reshape(1, -1).tocsr()
748	                inv = csr_self._scalar_binopt(other, op_neg[op])
749	                all_true = csr_self.__class__(np.ones(csr_self.shape, dtype=np.bool_))
750	                result = all_true - inv
751	                return result.tocoo().reshape(self.shape)
752	
753	        elif isdense(other):
754	            return op(self.todense(), other)
755	
756	        elif issparse(other):
757	            # TODO sparse broadcasting
758	            if self.shape != other.shape:
759	                # eq and ne return True or False instead of an array when the shapes
760	                # don't match. Numpy doesn't do this. Is this what we want?
761	                if op in (operator.eq, operator.ne):
762	                    return op is operator.ne
763	                raise ValueError("inconsistent shape")
764	
765	            if self.ndim < 3:
766	                cs_self = self if self.format in ('csc', 'csr') else self.tocsr()
767	                cs_other = other
768	            else:
769	                cs_self = self.reshape(1, -1).tocsr()
770	                cs_other = other.reshape(1, -1).tocsr()
771	            if not op(0, 0):
772	                result = cs_self._binopt(cs_other, f'_{op.__name__}_')
773	                return result if self.ndim < 3 else result.tocoo().reshape(self.shape)
774	            else:
775	                # result will not be sparse. Use negated op and then negate.
776	                warn(f"Comparing two sparse matrices using {op_sym[op]} "
777	                     f"is inefficient. Try using {op_sym[op_neg[op]]} instead.",
778	                     SparseEfficiencyWarning, stacklevel=3)
779	                inv = cs_self._binopt(cs_other, f'_{op_neg[op].__name__}_')
780	                all_true = cs_self.__class__(np.ones(cs_self.shape, dtype=np.bool_))
781	                result = all_true - inv
782	                return result if self.ndim < 3 else result.tocoo().reshape(self.shape)
783	        else:
784	            # cannot compare with other, but it might compare with us.
785	            return NotImplemented
786

788	        return self._comparison(other, operator.eq)

827	    def __add__(self, other):  # self + other
828	        if isscalarlike(other):
829	            if other == 0:
830	                return self.copy()
831	            # Now we would add this scalar to every element.
832	            raise NotImplementedError('adding a nonzero scalar to a '
833	                                      'sparse array is not supported')
834	        elif issparse(other):
835	            if other.shape != self.shape:
836	                raise ValueError("inconsistent shapes")
837	            return self._add_sparse(other)
838	        elif isdense(other):
839	            other = np.broadcast_to(other, self.shape)
840	            return self._add_dense(other)
841	        else:
842	            return NotImplemented

847	    def __sub__(self, other):  # self - other
848	        if isscalarlike(other):
849	            if other == 0:
850	                return self.copy()
851	            raise NotImplementedError('subtracting a nonzero scalar from a '
852	                                      'sparse array is not supported')
853	        elif issparse(other):
854	            if other.shape != self.shape:
855	                raise ValueError("inconsistent shapes")
856	            return self._sub_sparse(other)
857	        elif isdense(other):
858	            other = np.broadcast_to(other, self.shape)
859	            return self._sub_dense(other)

861	            return NotImplemented

863	    def __rsub__(self,other):  # other - self
864	        if isscalarlike(other):
865	            if other == 0:
866	                return -self.copy()
867	            raise NotImplementedError('subtracting a sparse array from a '
868	                                      'nonzero scalar is not supported')
869	        elif isdense(other):
870	            other = np.broadcast_to(other, self.shape)
871	            return self._rsub_dense(other)
872	        else:
873	            return NotImplemented

875	    def _matmul_dispatch(self, other):
876	        """np.array-like matmul & `np.matrix`-like mul, i.e. `dot` or `NotImplemented`
877	
878	        interpret other and call one of the following
879	        self._mul_scalar()
880	        self._matmul_vector()
881	        self._matmul_multivector()
882	        self._matmul_sparse()
883	        """
884	        # This method has to be different from `__matmul__` because it is also
885	        # called by sparse matrix classes.
886	
887	        # Currently matrix multiplication is only supported
888	        # for 2D arrays. Hence we unpacked and use only the
889	        # two last axes' lengths.
890	        M, N = self._shape_as_2d
891	
892	        if other.__class__ is np.ndarray:
893	            # Fast path for the most common case
894	            if other.shape == (N,):
895	                return self._matmul_vector(other)
896	            elif other.shape == (N, 1):
897	                result = self._matmul_vector(other.ravel())
898	                if self.ndim == 1:
899	                    return result.reshape(1)
900	                return result.reshape(M, 1)
901	            elif other.ndim == 2 and other.shape[0] == N:
902	                return self._matmul_multivector(other)
903	
904	        if isscalarlike(other):
905	            # scalar value
906	            return self._mul_scalar(other)
907	
908	        err_prefix = "matmul: dimension mismatch with signature"
909	        if issparse(other):
910	            if N != other.shape[0]:
911	                raise ValueError(
912	                    f"{err_prefix} (n,k={N}),(k={other.shape[0]},m)->(n,m)"
913	                )
914	            return self._matmul_sparse(other)
915	
916	        # If it's a list or whatever, treat it like an array
917	        other_a = np.asanyarray(other)
918	        if other_a.ndim == 0 and other_a.dtype == np.object_:
919	            # numpy creates a 0d object array if all else fails.
920	            # Not interpretable as an array; return NotImplemented so that
921	            # other's __rmatmul__ can kick in if that's implemented.
922	            return NotImplemented
923	        # Allow custom sparse class indicated by attr sparse gh-6520
924	        try:
925	            other.shape
926	        except AttributeError:
927	            other = other_a
928	
929	        if other.ndim == 1 or other.ndim == 2 and other.shape[1] == 1:
930	            # dense row or column vector
931	            if other.shape[0] != N:
932	                raise ValueError(
933	                    f"{err_prefix} (n,k={N}),(k={other.shape[0]},1?)->(n,1?)"
934	                )
935	
936	            result = self._matmul_vector(np.ravel(other))
937	
938	            if isinstance(other, np.matrix):
939	                result = self._ascontainer(result)
940	
941	            if other.ndim == 2 and other.shape[1] == 1:
942	                # If 'other' was an (nx1) column vector, reshape the result
943	                if self.ndim == 1:
944	                    result = result.reshape(1)
945	                else:
946	                    result = result.reshape(-1, 1)
947	
948	            return result
949	
950	        elif other.ndim == 2:
951	            ##
952	            # dense 2D array or matrix ("multivector")
953	
954	            if other.shape[0] != N:
955	                raise ValueError(
956	                    f"{err_prefix} (n,k={N}),(k={other.shape[0]},m)->(n,m)"
957	                )
958	
959	            result = self._matmul_multivector(np.asarray(other))
960	
961	            if isinstance(other, np.matrix):
962	                result = self._ascontainer(result)
963	
964	            return result
965	
966	        else:
967	            raise ValueError('could not interpret dimensions')
968

972	    def __rmul__(self, other):  # other * self
973	        return self.multiply(other)

975	    # by default, use CSR for __mul__ handlers
976	    def _mul_scalar(self, other):
977	        return self.tocsr()._mul_scalar(other)
978	
979	    def _matmul_vector(self, other):
980	        return self.tocsr()._matmul_vector(other)
981	
982	    def _matmul_multivector(self, other):
983	        return self.tocsr()._matmul_multivector(other)
984	
985	    def _matmul_sparse(self, other):
986	        return self.tocsr()._matmul_sparse(other)
987	
988	    def _rmatmul_dispatch(self, other):
989	        if isscalarlike(other):
990	            return self._mul_scalar(other)
991	        else:
992	            # Don't use asarray unless we have to
993	            try:
994	                tr = other.transpose()
995	            except AttributeError:
996	                tr = np.asarray(other).transpose()
997	            ret = self.transpose()._matmul_dispatch(tr)
998	            if ret is NotImplemented:
999	                return NotImplemented
1000	            return ret.transpose()

1022	    def _divide(self, other, *, rdivide=False):
1023	        if not (issparse(other) or isdense(other) or isscalarlike(other)):
1024	            # If it's a list or whatever, treat it like an array
1025	            other_a = np.asanyarray(other)
1026	            if other_a.ndim == 0 and other_a.dtype == np.object_:
1027	                # numpy creates a 0d object array if all else fails.
1028	                # Not interpretable as an array; return NotImplemented so that
1029	                # other's __rtruediv__ can kick in if that's implemented.
1030	                return NotImplemented
1031	            # Allow custom sparse class indicated by attr sparse gh-6520
1032	            try:
1033	                other.shape
1034	            except AttributeError:
1035	                other = other_a
1036	
1037	        if isscalarlike(other):
1038	            if rdivide:
1039	                return np.divide(other, self.todense())
1040	
1041	            if np.can_cast(self.dtype, np.float64):
1042	                return self.astype(np.float64, copy=False)._mul_scalar(1 / other)
1043	            else:
1044	                r = self._mul_scalar(1 / other)
1045	
1046	                scalar_dtype = np.asarray(other).dtype
1047	                if (np.issubdtype(self.dtype, np.integer) and
1048	                        np.issubdtype(scalar_dtype, np.integer)):
1049	                    return r.astype(self.dtype, copy=False)
1050	                else:
1051	                    return r
1052	
1053	        elif isdense(other):
1054	            if rdivide:
1055	                return np.divide(other, self.todense())
1056	
1057	            return self.multiply(np.divide(1, other))
1058	
1059	        elif issparse(other):
1060	            if rdivide:
1061	                return other._divide(self, rdivide=False)
1062	
1063	            csr_self = (self if self.ndim < 3 else self.reshape(1, -1)).tocsr()
1064	            csr_other = (other if self.ndim < 3 else other.reshape(1, -1)).tocsr()
1065	            if np.can_cast(self.dtype, np.float64):
1066	                csr_self = csr_self.astype(np.float64, copy=False)
1067	            result = csr_self._divide_sparse(csr_other)
1068	            return result if self.ndim < 3 else result.reshape(self.shape)
1069	        else:
1070	            # not scalar, dense or sparse. Return NotImplemented so
1071	            # other's __rtruediv__ can kick in if that's implemented.
1072	            return NotImplemented
1073	
1074	    def __truediv__(self, other):
1075	        return self._divide(other)

1078	        # Implementing this as the inverse would be too magical -- bail out
1079	        return NotImplemented

1099	    def transpose(self, axes=None, copy=False):
1100	        """
1101	        Reverses the dimensions of the sparse array/matrix.
1102	
1103	        Parameters
1104	        ----------
1105	        axes : None, optional
1106	            This argument is in the signature *solely* for NumPy
1107	            compatibility reasons. Do not pass in anything except
1108	            for the default value.
1109	        copy : bool, optional
1110	            Indicates whether or not attributes of `self` should be
1111	            copied whenever possible. The degree to which attributes
1112	            are copied varies depending on the type of sparse array/matrix
1113	            being used.
1114	
1115	        Returns
1116	        -------
1117	        p : sparse array/matrix
1118	            The transpose of the array/matrix.
1119	
1120	        See Also
1121	        --------

1165	    def nonzero(self):
1166	        """Nonzero indices of the array/matrix.
1167	
1168	        Returns
1169	        -------
1170	        row : ndarray
1171	            Row indices of non-zero elements.
1172	        col : ndarray
1173	            Column indices of non-zero elements.
1174	
1175	        Examples
1176	        --------
1177	        >>> from scipy.sparse import csr_array
1178	        >>> A = csr_array([[1, 2, 0], [0, 0, 3], [4, 0, 5]])
1179	        >>> A.nonzero()
1180	        (array([0, 0, 1, 2, 2], dtype=int32), array([0, 1, 2, 0, 2], dtype=int32))

1189	    def _getcol(self, j, /):
1190	        """Returns a copy of column j of the array, as an (m x 1) sparse
1191	        array (column vector).
1192	        """
1193	        if self.ndim == 1:
1194	            raise ValueError("getcol not provided for 1d arrays. Use indexing A[j]")
1195	        # Subclasses should override this method for efficiency.
1196	        # Post-multiply by a (n x 1) column vector 'a' containing all zeros
1197	        # except for a_j = 1
1198	        N = self.shape[-1]
1199	        if j < 0:
1200	            j += N
1201	        if j < 0 or j >= N:
1202	            raise IndexError("index out of bounds")
1203	        col_selector = self._csc_container(([1], [[j], [0]]),
1204	                                           shape=(N, 1), dtype=self.dtype)
1205	        result = self @ col_selector
1206	        return result

1208	    def _getrow(self, i):
1209	        """Returns a copy of row i of the array, as a (1 x n) sparse
1210	        array (row vector).
1211	        """
1212	        if self.ndim == 1:
1213	            raise ValueError("getrow not meaningful for a 1d array")
1214	        # Subclasses should override this method for efficiency.
1215	        # Pre-multiply by a (1 x m) row vector 'a' containing all zeros
1216	        # except for a_i = 1
1217	        M = self.shape[0]
1218	        if i < 0:
1219	            i += M
1220	        if i < 0 or i >= M:
1221	            raise IndexError("index out of bounds")
1222	        row_selector = self._csr_container(([1], [[0], [i]]),
1223	                                           shape=(1, M), dtype=self.dtype)
1224	        return row_selector @ self

1246	    def todense(self, order=None, out=None):
1247	        """
1248	        Return a dense representation of this sparse array.
1249	
1250	        Parameters
1251	        ----------
1252	        order : {'C', 'F'}, optional
1253	            Whether to store multi-dimensional data in C (row-major)
1254	            or Fortran (column-major) order in memory. The default
1255	            is 'None', which provides no ordering guarantees.
1256	            Cannot be specified in conjunction with the `out`
1257	            argument.

1259	        out : ndarray, 2-D, optional
1260	            If specified, uses this array as the output buffer
1261	            instead of allocating a new array to return. The
1262	            provided array must have the same shape and dtype as
1263	            the sparse array on which you are calling the method.

1269	            data represented by the sparse array, with the requested
1270	            memory order. If `out` was passed, the same object is
1271	            returned after being modified in-place to contain the
1272	            appropriate values.

1439	    def sum(self, axis=None, dtype=None, out=None):
1440	        """
1441	        Sum the array/matrix elements over a given axis.
1442	
1443	        Parameters
1444	        ----------
1445	        axis : {-2, -1, 0, 1, None} optional
1446	            Axis along which the sum is computed. The default is to
1447	            compute the sum of all the array/matrix elements, returning a scalar
1448	            (i.e., `axis` = `None`).
1449	        dtype : dtype, optional
1450	            The type of the returned array/matrix and of the accumulator in which
1451	            the elements are summed.  The dtype of `a` is used by default
1452	            unless `a` has an integer dtype of less precision than the default
1453	            platform integer.  In that case, if `a` is signed then the platform
1454	            integer is used while if `a` is unsigned then an unsigned integer
1455	            of the same precision as the platform integer is used.
1456	
1457	            .. versionadded:: 0.18.0
1458	
1459	        out : np.matrix, optional
1460	            Alternative output matrix in which to place the result. It must
1461	            have the same shape as the expected output, but the type of the
1462	            output values will be cast if necessary.
1463	
1464	            .. versionadded:: 0.18.0
1465	
1466	        Returns
1467	        -------
1468	        sum_along_axis : np.matrix
1469	            A matrix with the same shape as `self`, with the specified
1470	            axis removed.
1471	
1472	        See Also
1473	        --------
1474	        numpy.matrix.sum : NumPy's implementation of 'sum' for matrices
1475	
1476	        """
1477	        axis = validateaxis(axis, ndim=self.ndim)
1478	
1479	        # Mimic numpy's casting.
1480	        res_dtype = get_sum_dtype(self.dtype)
1481	
1482	        if dtype is not None:
1483	            # Before casting to the requested dtype, canonicalize duplicates and zeros.
1484	            if hasattr(self, 'sum_duplicates'):
1485	                self.sum_duplicates()
1486	            temp = self.astype(dtype, copy=False).sum(axis=axis, dtype=None, out=out)
1487	            return temp.astype(dtype, copy=False)
1488	
1489	        # Note: all valid 1D axis values are canonically `None`.
1490	        if axis is None:
1491	            if self.nnz == 0:
1492	                return np.sum(self._ascontainer([0]), dtype=dtype or res_dtype, out=out)
1493	            return np.sum(self._ascontainer(_todata(self)), dtype=dtype, out=out)
1494	        elif isinstance(self, sparray):
1495	            new_shape = tuple(self.shape[i] for i in range(self.ndim) if i not in axis)
1496	        else:
1497	            # Ensure spmatrix sums stay 2D
1498	            new_shape = (1, self.shape[1]) if axis == (0,) else (self.shape[0], 1)
1499	
1500	        if out is None:
1501	            # create out array with desired dtype
1502	            out = self._ascontainer(np.zeros(new_shape, dtype=dtype or res_dtype))
1503	        else:
1504	            if out.shape != new_shape:
1505	                raise ValueError("out dimensions do not match shape")
1506	
1507	        if self.ndim > 2:
1508	            return self._sum_nd(axis, res_dtype, out)
1509	
1510	        # We use multiplication by a matrix of ones to sum.
1511	        # For some sparse array formats more efficient methods are
1512	        # possible -- these should override this function.
1513	        if axis == (0,):
1514	            ones = self._ascontainer(np.ones((1, self.shape[0]), dtype=res_dtype))
1515	            # sets dtype while loading into out
1516	            out[...] = (ones @ self).reshape(new_shape)
1517	        else:  # axis == (1,)
1518	            ones = self._ascontainer(np.ones((self.shape[1], 1), dtype=res_dtype))
1519	            # sets dtype while loading into out
1520	            out[...] = (self @ ones).reshape(new_shape)
1521	        return out
1522	
1523	    def mean(self, axis=None, dtype=None, out=None):
1524	        """
1525	        Compute the arithmetic mean along the specified axis.
1526	
1527	        Returns the average of the array/matrix elements. The average is taken
1528	        over all elements in the array/matrix by default, otherwise over the
1529	        specified axis. `float64` intermediate and return values are used
1530	        for integer inputs.
1531	
1532	        Parameters
1533	        ----------
1534	        axis : {-2, -1, 0, 1, None} optional
1535	            Axis along which the mean is computed. The default is to compute
1536	            the mean of all elements in the array/matrix (i.e., `axis` = `None`).
1537	        dtype : data-type, optional
1538	            Type to use in computing the mean. For integer inputs, the default
1539	            is `float64`; for floating point inputs, it is the same as the
1540	            input dtype.
1541	
1542	            .. versionadded:: 0.18.0
1543	
1544	        out : np.matrix, optional
1545	            Alternative output matrix in which to place the result. It must
1546	            have the same shape as the expected output, but the type of the
1547	            output values will be cast if necessary.
1548	
1549	            .. versionadded:: 0.18.0
1550	
1551	        Returns
1552	        -------
1553	        m : np.matrix
1554	            Mean along the specified axis.
1555	
1556	        See Also
1557	        --------
1558	        numpy.matrix.mean : NumPy's implementation of 'mean' for matrices
1559	
1560	        """
1561	        axis = validateaxis(axis, ndim=self.ndim)
1562	
1563	        integral = (np.issubdtype(self.dtype, np.integer) or
1564	                    np.issubdtype(self.dtype, np.bool_))
1565	
1566	        # intermediate dtype for summation
1567	        inter_dtype = np.float64 if integral else self.dtype
1568	        inter_self = self.astype(inter_dtype, copy=False)
1569	
1570	        if axis is None:
1571	            denom = math.prod(self.shape)
1572	        else:
1573	            denom = math.prod(self.shape[ax] for ax in axis)
1574	        res = (inter_self * (1.0 / denom)).sum(axis=axis, dtype=inter_dtype, out=out)
1575	        if dtype is not None and out is None:
1576	            return res.astype(dtype, copy=False)
1577	        return res

1580	    def diagonal(self, k=0):
1581	        """Returns the kth diagonal of the array/matrix.
1582	
1583	        Parameters
1584	        ----------
1585	        k : int, optional
1586	            Which diagonal to get, corresponding to elements a[i, i+k].
1587	            Default: 0 (the main diagonal).
1588	
1589	            .. versionadded:: 1.0
1590	
1591	        Returns
1592	        -------
1593	        d : ndarray
1594	            The specified diagonal of the array/matrix.
1595	
1596	        See Also
1597	        --------
1598	        numpy.diagonal : Equivalent numpy function.
1599	
1600	        Examples
1601	        --------
1602	        >>> from scipy.sparse import csr_array
1603	        >>> A = csr_array([[1, 2, 0], [0, 0, 3], [4, 0, 5]])
1604	        >>> A.diagonal()
1605	        array([1, 0, 5])
1606	        >>> A.diagonal(k=1)
1607	        array([2, 3])
1608	        """
1609	        return self.tocsr().diagonal(k=k)

1611	    def trace(self, offset=0):
1612	        """Returns the sum along diagonals of the sparse array/matrix.
1613	
1614	        Parameters
1615	        ----------
1616	        offset : int, optional
1617	            Which diagonal to get, corresponding to elements a[i, i+offset].
1618	            Default: 0 (the main diagonal).
1619	
1620	        Returns
1621	        -------
1622	        scalar
1623	            The sum of the specified diagonal of the array/matrix.
1624	        """

1627	    def setdiag(self, values, k=0):
1628	        """
1629	        Set diagonal or off-diagonal elements of the array/matrix.
1630	
1631	        Parameters
1632	        ----------
1633	        values : array_like
1634	            New values of the diagonal elements.
1635	
1636	            Values may have any length. If the diagonal is longer than values,
1637	            then the remaining diagonal entries will not be set. If values are
1638	            longer than the diagonal, then the remaining values are ignored.
1639	
1640	            If a scalar value is given, all of the diagonal is set to it.
1641	
1642	        k : int, optional
1643	            Which off-diagonal to set, corresponding to elements a[i,i+k].
1644	            Default: 0 (the main diagonal).
1645	
1646	        """
1647	        M, N = self.shape
1648	        if (k > 0 and k >= N) or (k < 0 and -k >= M):
1649	            raise ValueError("k exceeds array dimensions")
1650	        self._setdiag(np.asarray(values), k)
1651	
1652	    def _setdiag(self, values, k):
1653	        """This part of the implementation gets overridden by the
1654	        different formats.
1655	        """
1656	        M, N = self.shape
1657	        if k < 0:
1658	            if values.ndim == 0:
1659	                # broadcast
1660	                max_index = min(M+k, N)
1661	                for i in range(max_index):
1662	                    self[i - k, i] = values
1663	            else:
1664	                max_index = min(M+k, N, len(values))
1665	                if max_index <= 0:
1666	                    return
1667	                for i, v in enumerate(values[:max_index]):
1668	                    self[i - k, i] = v
1669	        else:
1670	            if values.ndim == 0:
1671	                # broadcast
1672	                max_index = min(M, N-k)
1673	                for i in range(max_index):
1674	                    self[i, i + k] = values
1675	            else:
1676	                max_index = min(M, N-k, len(values))
1677	                if max_index <= 0:
1678	                    return
1679	                for i, v in enumerate(values[:max_index]):
1680	                    self[i, i + k] = v

1682	    def _process_toarray_args(self, order, out):
1683	        if out is not None:
1684	            if order is not None:
1685	                raise ValueError('order cannot be specified if out '
1686	                                 'is not None')
1687	            if out.shape != self.shape or out.dtype != self.dtype:
1688	                raise ValueError('out array must be same dtype and shape as '
1689	                                 'sparse array')
1690	            out[...] = 0.
1691	            return out
1692	        else:
1693	            return np.zeros(self.shape, dtype=self.dtype, order=order)

1695	    def _get_index_dtype(self, arrays=(), maxval=None, check_contents=False):
1696	        """
1697	        Determine index dtype for array.
1698	
1699	        This wraps _sputils.get_index_dtype, providing compatibility for both
1700	        array and matrix API sparse matrices. Matrix API sparse matrices would
1701	        attempt to downcast the indices - which can be computationally
1702	        expensive and undesirable for users. The array API changes this
1703	        behaviour.
1704	
1705	        See discussion: https://github.com/scipy/scipy/issues/16774
1706	
1707	        The get_index_dtype import is due to implementation details of the test
1708	        suite. It allows the decorator ``with_64bit_maxval_limit`` to mock a
1709	        lower int32 max value for checks on the matrix API's downcasting
1710	        behaviour.
1711	        """
1712	        from ._sputils import get_index_dtype
1713	
1714	        # Don't check contents for array API
1715	        return get_index_dtype(arrays,
1716	                               maxval,
1717	                               (check_contents and not isinstance(self, sparray)))

1720	class sparray:
1721	    """A namespace class to separate sparray from spmatrix.
1722	
1723	    This class serves as the namespace for SciPy sparse array types.
1724	    It cannot be instantiated and is designed as a mixin class.
1725	    """

1727	    @classmethod
1728	    def __class_getitem__(cls, arg, /):
1729	        """
1730	        Return a parametrized wrapper around the `~scipy.sparse.sparray` type.
1731	
1732	        .. versionadded:: 1.16.0

1734	        Returns
1735	        -------
1736	        alias : types.GenericAlias
1737	            A parametrized `~scipy.sparse.sparray` type.
1738	
1739	        Examples
1740	        --------
1741	        >>> import numpy as np
1742	        >>> from scipy.sparse import coo_array
1743	
1744	        >>> coo_array[np.int8, tuple[int]]
1745	        scipy.sparse._coo.coo_array[numpy.int8, tuple[int]]
1746	        """
1747	        from types import GenericAlias
1748	        return GenericAlias(cls, arg)
1749	
1750	
1751	sparray.__doc__ = _spbase.__doc__
1752	
1753	
1754	def isspmatrix(x):
1755	    """Is `x` of a sparse matrix type?
1756	
1757	    .. warning::
1758	
1759	        scipy.sparse is switching to the sparse array interface.
1760	
1761	        The ``isspmatrix`` function returns ``False`` for sparse arrays.
1762	        It will remain after the switch to sparse arrays (sparray).
1763	        So this is a future proof way to check for sparray vs spmatrix.
1764	        If you just want to check for sparse, use ``issparse(A)``.
1765	        For more general information about sparrays, see
1766	        :ref:`Migration from spmatrix to sparray <migration_to_sparray>`.
1767	        The switch to sparse arrays will occur no earlier than v1.21.
1768	
1769	    Parameters
1770	    ----------
1771	    x
1772	        object to check for being a sparse matrix
1773	
1774	    Returns
1775	    -------
1776	    bool
1777	        True if `x` is a sparse matrix, False otherwise
1778	
1779	    Examples
1780	    --------
1781	    >>> import numpy as np
1782	    >>> from scipy.sparse import csr_array, csr_matrix, isspmatrix
1783	    >>> isspmatrix(csr_matrix([[5]]))
1784	    True
1785	    >>> isspmatrix(csr_array([[5]]))
1786	    False
1787	    >>> isspmatrix(np.array([[5]]))
1788	    False
1789	    >>> isspmatrix(5)
1790	    False
